title: Totem Resize
description: The Totem Resizer is a client-side mod that lets you change the size of the "Totem of Undying" in your hand and on your screen. It’s perfect for clearing up your view during combat or just personalizing your game.
body:
TotemScale is a sleek, lightweight, client-side utility designed to solve one of Minecraft’s oldest visual nuisances: the bulky "Totem of Undying" model. Whether you are a PVPer who needs a clear field of view or a casual player who wants a more minimalist aesthetic, TotemScale gives you total control over how the totem appears on your screen.

Built for the 1.21.x era, this mod integrates directly into the modern Minecraft engine, ensuring high performance and a "native" feel.

## Key Features
Precision Scaling (0.1x – 2.5x): Use an intuitive in-game slider to shrink the totem into a tiny charm or grow it into a massive icon.

Total Visual Coverage: The scaling logic applies to every state of the totem:

Main Hand: Keeps your right-side vision clear.

Off-Hand: Prevents the totem from blocking your peripheral view.

The "Pop" Animation: Shrinks the large on-screen animation that triggers when you cheat death, so you can keep fighting without a giant golden face in your way.

Fully Rebindable Controls: No more digging through config files. Access the customization menu with a single keypress (Default: 'J'). The keybinding is fully integrated into the standard Minecraft Options > Controls menu.

Vanilla & Server Compatible: Since the mod only changes how you see the totem, it is 100% Client-Side. You can use it on any vanilla server, SMP, or competitive network (like Hypixel) without any risk or extra server-side setup.

### Technical Excellence
Modern Compatibility: Optimized for Java 21 and the 1.21.1 through 1.21.11 update cycle.

Data Component Ready: Uses the latest 1.21 item handling system, ensuring it works perfectly with custom resource packs and modded totems.

Seamless UI: Powered by a clean, modern interface that matches the updated 1.21 "Tricky Trials" and "Mounts of Mayhem" menu styles.

Zero Impact: Lightweight code ensures your FPS remains stable, even during intense combat or "totem popping" scenarios.
